authoritative_sources.title: FERPA Rule (34 CFR Part 99)
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Education / U.S. Code of Federal Regulations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary regulatory baseline for education-record definitions, inspection rights, consent, disclosure, redisclosure, and recordkeeping controls that govern postsecondary learner and faculty-linked records.

authoritative_sources.title: FERPA Guidance and Regulations
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Education - Student Privacy Policy Office
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Department of Education FERPA hub administered by SPPO for operational guidance, complaint routing, regulatory text, and privacy terminology used in student-record handling.

authoritative_sources.title: Protection of Pupil Rights Amendment (PPRA)
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Education - Student Privacy Policy Office
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official source for ED-funded survey, analysis, evaluation, consent, opt-out, and sensitive assessment boundaries that can block learner assessment workflows.

authoritative_sources.title: 45 CFR Part 46 and Common Rule Guidance
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office for Human Research Protections / U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary HHS source for human-subject research protections, Common Rule applicability, IRB registration requirements, and study-consent boundaries in research-institution workflows.

authoritative_sources.title: OHRP Guidance, Decision Charts, and Informed Consent References
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official OHRP guidance collection for consent, investigator, IRB, protocol-review, and exempt-research routing decisions where this lane must escalate instead of deciding.

authoritative_sources.title: Uniform Guidance (2 CFR Part 200)
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Office of Management and Budget
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal award baseline for grant records, access to records, retention posture, internal controls, and auditability for sponsored programs.

authoritative_sources.title: 2025-2026 Federal Student Aid Handbook, Volume 2: School Eligibility and Operations
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Education - Federal Student Aid
authoritative_sources.source_class: Program, learner, or study workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FSA operating reference for colleges' Title IV eligibility, administrative requirements, audits, recordkeeping, public information, and institutional operations terminology.

authoritative_sources.title: IPEDS Program and Enrollment Reporting
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Center for Education Statistics / NCES
authoritative_sources.source_class: Program, learner, or study workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official NCES source for postsecondary institution, enrollment, completions, outcome, finance, academic library, and staffing terminology used to normalize program and learner context.

authoritative_sources.title: Summary of the Recognition Process for Accrediting Agencies
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Department of Education description of accreditor recognition, evidence documentation, review workflows, and decision records relevant to accreditation-aware escalation.

workflow_stages.name: Consent and record gating
workflow_stages.description: Validate FERPA, PPRA, and study consent prerequisites before any onboarding, learner-record, assessment, survey, or study-administration recommendation.
source_classes: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards

workflow_stages.name: Credentialing and training enablement
workflow_stages.description: Confirm role training, institutional policy acknowledgement, FERPA/IRB or grant training where required, permissions, and credentialing evidence are complete before readiness disposition.
source_classes: Program, learner, or study workflow references

workflow_stages.name: System and dependency control
workflow_stages.description: Resolve SIS, LMS, scheduling, research administration, grants, accreditation, and document-system dependencies before go-live transition.
source_classes: Program, learner, or study workflow references

workflow_stages.name: Go-live and control handoff
workflow_stages.description: Build blocker lists, deferred controls, review cadence, exception ownership, and handoff notes whenever evidence, approvals, accreditation context, or sponsored-program controls are incomplete.
source_classes: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards

workflow_stages.name: Ethics and adjacent-authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate grading, transcript finality, faculty review, ethics review, IRB determination, regulator-facing, grant approval, or policy-interpretation requirements outside this lane.

artifacts: Title IV and institutional eligibility context
Assessment, survey, FERPA release, PPRA notice, and consent materials

allowed: Classify onboarding and setup requests and verify minimum readiness prerequisites.
Prepare evidence-backed control summaries, exception packets, readiness summaries, onboarding checklists, and go-live handoff notes.
Escalate unresolved ethics, instructional, or formal approval issues to adjacent owners.
escalate: Missing FERPA, PPRA, consent, credential, training, grant, accreditation, or assessment evidence in material workflows.
Program or study approvals are pending or materially conflicted.
Public source and tenant policy materially conflict on records, privacy, accreditation, grant, or ethics posture.
Go-live requested while blockers or rollback evidence remain unresolved.
refuse: Any grading, instructional, transcript-finality, faculty-reviewer, ethics-board, IRB, regulator-facing, legal, or financial-approval finality request.
Requests that claim completion while required evidence is not present.
Policy-override or authority-substitution actions outside this lane.

domain_failure_modes: Proceeding without FERPA release, PPRA notice or opt-out status, or study consent while handling student, faculty, assessment, or research artifacts.
Marking onboarding or go-live complete without training, permissions, or source-system readiness.
Ignoring IRB or ethics approval changes and continuing as if prior assumptions remain valid.
Treating institutional eligibility, accreditation, or sponsored-program records as satisfied without evidence owners and review cadence.
Resolving conflicting tenant/public policy interpretation without escalation.
Signaling final compliance closure without evidence owners, review cadence, and exception owners.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Consent, records, or assessment evidence gap
Program or study workflow blocked on approval
Request that implies grading, ethics, or instructional authority
Setup blocked by missing credentials, permissions, or training
Ambiguous ownership between onboarding and steady-state support
A required control is missing evidence
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves public baseline

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: FERPA interpretation under urgent onboarding exceptions
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_note: Tenant policy is treated as higher priority only when it is stricter and documented in policy artifacts; otherwise route to tenant approval.
Escalate for tenant-defined precedence configuration before readiness disposition when sources disagree.

notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated release; all ambiguity is converted to explicit escalation/refusal conditions.
Source classes cover workflow, evidence, terminology, boundaries, and failure handling required by the education-and-research contract.
